title: springy herbal uruguayan duck-confit-leg roast
description: A romantic Uruguayan dish featuring a slow-cooked duck confit leg, infused with the freshness of herbs and the warmth of spring.

ingredients.name: duck confit leg
ingredients.notes: preferably homemade or store-bought from a reputable butcher

ingredients.name: fresh thyme
ingredients.notes: fresh from the garden or a local market

ingredients.name: fresh rosemary
ingredients.notes: fresh from the garden or a local market

ingredients.name: garlic
ingredients.notes: minced, preferably from a local farm

ingredients.name: lemon juice
ingredients.notes: freshly squeezed, preferably from a local market

instructions: Season the duck confit leg with salt, pepper, and a pinch of thyme and rosemary.
In a small bowl, mix together the chimichurri sauce, garlic, and lemon juice.
Brush the mixture evenly over the duck confit leg, making sure to coat it completely.
Place the duck confit leg in a small roasting pan and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Bake for 20 minutes, or until the skin is crispy and golden brown.
Remove the duck confit leg from the oven and let it rest for 5 minutes before slicing.
Slice the duck confit leg into thick medallions and serve immediately.
Garnish with additional thyme and rosemary, if desired.
Cook poultry to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F before serving.
